description: This figure presents a line plot quantifying the intragranular κ-carbide precipitate size as a function of aging temperature for Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel subjected to 6 h of aging. The horizontal axis indicates aging temperature at 550, 600, and 650 °C, while the vertical axis gives the intragranular κ-carbide size from 0 to 30 nm. A single data series, labeled 'Aged for 6 h,' plots mean values connected by a straight line, with vertical error bars representing standard deviation as noted in the caption. The measured precipitate sizes increase monotonically and approximately linearly with rising temperature: from roughly 11 nm at 550 °C, to about 18 nm at 600 °C, and further to approximately 24 nm at 650 °C. This systematic coarsening behavior indicates that elevated aging temperatures accelerate the growth of intragranular κ-carbides while maintaining their morphology. The figure supports the core scientific conclusion that aging temperature is a critical processing parameter controlling κ-carbide dimensions, thereby establishing the microstructural basis for interpreting the non-monotonic fatigue property variations reported for this alloy system.
